review_english: Fall Guys where to start? It's clearly one of my favorite battle royales and yet, it has absolutely nothing to do with what the genre usually offers. Here, no guns, no build, no pro-gamer reflexes just having pure fun, chaos while hoping to survive one more round. And honestly? It works incredibly well. The gameplay is both ultra accessible and subtly demanding. You can start a game in relaxation mode, laugh at your falls but also try hard on certain maps when you really want to achieve victory. There is a real build in tension with each round, especially when you reach the final. And that, without ever becoming frustrating or unfair (well except when you get pushed into the void at the last second, but that's part of the charm')). What I've always loved about Fall Guys is how refreshing it is. At a time when all battle royales were more or less the same, he dared to offer something totally different. And it directly reminded me of another game that I really liked, namely Darwin Project. Two very different games, but with this same feeling of novelty, of originality, of well, it's finally changing and it's ultra pleasant. But beyond the gameplay, Fall Guys is above all a huge memory machine. Since 2020, I have spent a lot of time there. Whole days, endless evenings with friends, playing one more game after another, saying let's go for one more time... only to end up spending hours there. Moments of rage but above all uncontrollable laughter. Absurd failures, improbable victories, finals lost by an inch. It's this kind of game that creates moments that you won't forget. And the crazy thing is that even today, I still enjoy it just as much. There is this simplicity, this efficiency in the gameplay which means that the game does not really age. You restart a game, and you instantly regain the sensations. But inevitably over time, there is a little melancholy that sets in. We feel that the game is played less than before. The years go by, players leave, fashions evolve even if the game is still there, it has lost a little of its original magic. I'm not stupid, one day the servers will close. And that day, it will really mean something to me. Because Fall Guys is a game that I sincerely loved, from start to finish. A huge favorite. I also have this feeling, a little bitter, that the takeover by Epic Games did not necessarily help the game to keep its soul, on the contrary I find that it was a little distorted on certain points and the trials were no longer at all as interesting as the previous seasons, there is perhaps season 1 with Epic Games which was nice but after that it became less and less good. And seeing copies like Stumble Guys take up more and more space is weird, especially when you know how unique Fall Guys was when it came out. So, I set myself a personal, almost symbolic goal: to finish the game at 100 in terms of success before it disappears one day. And recently I did it. Honestly, it did something to me. Because it wasn't simple at all. There were moments of struggle, frustrating challenges, parts where you wonder why you are doing this to yourself, but in the end, enormous satisfaction. A real feeling of accomplishment. I'm just happy to have been able to complete this adventure. To have finished this game that accompanied me for years, before one day, perhaps, we had to say goodbye to it. So really, if you have the opportunity to play it again, do it. Start one game, then two, then ten. Enjoy it with your friends, laugh, fall, lose, win because there aren't many games like Fall Guys. And when he disappears one day, he will leave behind something rare, incredible memories. A huge crush. Always. Thanks Fall Guys. Thanks Mediatonic. 3

*DENIED*

*Uninstall game and disappointed for what I just discovered*

review:
[h1]「どけよ！！モブどもが！！」
なお次の瞬間、自分も落ちる。[/h1]

フレンドと遊ぶとめちゃくちゃ楽しいゲーム。

最初は
「かわいいパーティーゲームかな？」
くらいの気持ちで始めたのに、
気づけば全員叫んでる。

特にレース系ステージ。

前に大量のプレイヤーがいると、

「邪魔！！！」
「押すな！！！」
「どけよモブどもが！！」

が自然に飛び出す。

でも、
そうやってイキった直後に
自分もハンマーで吹き飛ばされる。

このゲーム、
ずっとそれの繰り返し。

あと物理演算が絶妙。

操作がちょっとフニャフニャしてるせいで、
真面目にやってるのに絵面がずっと面白い。
転ぶだけで笑える。

運ゲー寄りのステージもあるけど、
逆にそれがパーティーゲームとしてかなり良い。

ガチ実力勝負すぎないから、
初心者混じりでも普通に盛り上がれる。

あと終盤になると、
さっきまで笑ってた空気が急に変わる。

「ここまで来たら勝ちたい」

が全員に芽生える。

勝てばめちゃくちゃ嬉しいし、
負けてもなんだかんだ笑える。
“友達と騒ぎながら遊ぶゲーム”としてかなり強かった。

review_english: h1 Get out of the way, the mobs, and the next moment you fall too. h1 It's a really fun game to play with friends.At first, I thought it was a cute party game, but before I knew it, everyone was screaming.Especially in racing stages.When there are a lot of players in front of you, it gets in the way.Don't push me.Get out of the way, the mobs jump out on their own.But right after you do that, you get blown away by a hammer.This game keeps repeating itself.Also, the physics are exquisite. The controls are a bit fluffy, so even though you're playing seriously, the pictures are always interesting. Just falling makes you laugh. There are some stages that are more like a luck game, but on the other hand, it's pretty good as a party game. It's not too much of a test of skill, so even beginners can have a good time. Also, towards the end, the laughing atmosphere suddenly changes. After getting this far, everyone starts to want to win. If you win, you'll be really happy, and if you lose, it's still funny. It was a pretty strong game to play while making noise with friends.

review:
Fall Guys is one of those games that found its audience by being genuinely accessible without dumbing down the fun for people who play games regularly. The chaos is the point and it scales to whoever is in the room, which is a harder design balance to strike than it looks. Getting knocked off a platform by a giant spinning hammer is funny whether you've been gaming for thirty years or picked up a controller for the first time.

The variety in the minigame rotation keeps it from going stale quickly. Some rounds are pure chaos with minimal skill expression, others reward spatial awareness and timing, and the mix means no two sessions feel identical. For a party game that's the most important thing to get right and Mediatonic largely got it right from the start.

The free to play transition brought the expected complications with it. The cosmetic store is aggressive and the seasonal content model means the game is constantly pushing you toward spending, which is noticeable enough that it colors the experience even if you never open your wallet. For a game this good at being welcoming it's an unfortunate friction point that works against what makes it appealing in the first place.

The absence of split screen is the most glaring omission for exactly the use case this game is built for. Sitting in the same room with family and friends is the natural context for something like this, and having to either share a single screen awkwardly or set up multiple devices to play together is a real barrier that Mediatonic has never adequately addressed. For a party game that oversight is hard to overlook.

Still one of the easier recommendations for getting non-gamers into the same game as everyone else, which is genuinely valuable.

Avaliação: Fall Guys – Excelente Custo-Benefício e Mecânicas de Jogo
Nota: 5/5

Fall Guys se consolidou como um dos melhores exemplos de como o modelo Free-to-Play pode ser bem executado. Como alguém que prioriza análise de dados e eficiência em entretenimento, destaco os seguintes pontos:

Mecânicas de Colisão e Física: O jogo utiliza um sistema de física previsível, mas caótico na medida certa, o que exige um raciocínio lógico rápido para prever trajetórias e movimentos dos adversários.

Progressão e Monetização: O sistema de passes e cosméticos é justo. É perfeitamente possível desfrutar de 100% da experiência competitiva sem aportes financeiros recorrentes, o que eleva o ROI (Retorno sobre Investimento) de tempo do jogador.

Curva de Aprendizado: O design das fases permite uma entrada fácil para iniciantes, mas reserva táticas avançadas (como frames de pulo e otimização de rota) para quem busca um desempenho de alto nível.

Performance: O jogo é bem otimizado, mantendo estabilidade de FPS mesmo com muitos elementos simultâneos na tela, garantindo a confiabilidade necessária para partidas competitivas.

Veredito: Um software robusto, com atualizações constantes e uma proposta de valor imbatível no mercado atual de jogos casuais/competitivos. Recomendo para quem busca diversão estratégica e rápida.

review_english: Fall Guys Review Excellent Value for Money and Game Mechanics Rating 5 5 Fall Guys has established itself as one of the best examples of how the Free-to-Play model can be well executed. As someone who prioritizes data analysis and efficiency in entertainment, I highlight the following points Collision Mechanics and Physics The game uses a predictable but chaotic physics system, which requires quick logical reasoning to predict opponents' trajectories and movements. Progression and Monetization The pass and cosmetics system is fair. It is perfectly possible to enjoy 100% of the competitive experience without recurring financial contributions, which increases the ROI (Return on Investment) of the player's time. Learning Curve The level design allows for easy entry for beginners, but reserves advanced tactics (such as jump frames and route optimization) for those looking for high-level performance. Performance The game is well optimized, maintaining FPS stability even with many simultaneous elements on the screen, ensuring the reliability necessary for competitive matches. Verdict Robust software, with constant updates and an unbeatable value proposition in the current competitive casual games market. I recommend it for anyone looking for strategic and quick fun.

Fall Guys sigue siendo uno de esos juegos que, incluso en 2026, mantiene algo que muchos juegos competitivos han perdido: diversión pura y simple.

Puede que ya no tenga el mismo boom que cuando salió, pero el juego sigue siendo increíblemente entretenido. Las rondas son rápidas, caóticas y siempre pasan cosas absurdas que terminan en risas, ya sea porque alguien te empuja justo antes de la meta o porque todo el lobby cae al mismo agujero.

Lo mejor es que es un juego fácil de entender pero difícil de dominar. Cada partida es diferente y siempre hay momentos inesperados. Además, sigue recibiendo eventos, cosméticos y temporadas que mantienen el juego fresco.

review_english: Fall Guys is still one of those games that, even in 2026, maintains something that many competitive games have lost outright fun. It may not have the same buzz as when it came out, but the game is still incredibly entertaining. The rounds are fast, chaotic, and absurd things always happen that end in laughter, either because someone pushes you right before the finish line or because the entire lobby falls into the same hole. The best thing is that it is a game that is easy to understand but difficult to master. Each game is different and there are always unexpected moments. Plus, keep getting events, cosmetics, and seasons that keep the game fresh.

review:
Fall Guys, developed and published by Mediatonic, is a multiplayer party game that takes the familiar structure of a battle royale and transforms it into something far more playful and accessible. Instead of focusing on combat or survival in the traditional sense, it places dozens of players into a sequence of chaotic obstacle courses and mini-games, where the goal is simply to outlast everyone else. The premise is disarmingly simple, but the execution is what makes it stand out. By removing violence and emphasizing physics-driven chaos, the game creates an experience that feels more like a colorful game show than a competitive arena.

Each match unfolds across several rounds, with players gradually eliminated until only one remains. The variety of challenges is one of the game’s defining strengths. Some rounds are straightforward races to the finish line, while others involve surviving hazards, collecting objects, or competing in team-based activities. The unpredictability of these events keeps matches fresh, as players must constantly adapt to different mechanics and layouts. Even familiar stages can feel different depending on player behavior, as the sheer number of participants ensures that no two runs play out exactly the same. This constant variation is key to maintaining the game’s energy, making every session feel lively and dynamic.

What makes Fall Guys especially effective is how approachable it is. The controls are intentionally minimal, allowing players to jump, dive, and grab with ease. This simplicity means that anyone can pick up the game and understand its basics within minutes. At the same time, there is room for improvement and mastery. Experienced players learn how to navigate obstacles more efficiently, time their movements precisely, and position themselves strategically in crowded situations. However, the game never fully abandons its chaotic nature, ensuring that even the most skilled players are not immune to unexpected setbacks. This balance between skill and randomness creates a unique dynamic where success feels rewarding but never guaranteed.

The visual presentation plays a significant role in shaping the overall experience. The game’s bright, cartoon-like aesthetic is instantly recognizable, with vibrant colors and exaggerated designs that give it a distinct identity. The characters themselves, with their rounded shapes and wobbly movements, contribute to the game’s humor. Watching dozens of these characters stumble, collide, and tumble through obstacles creates a sense of physical comedy that is central to the game’s appeal. The environments, inspired by obstacle courses and playful set pieces, reinforce this tone, making every stage feel like part of a whimsical competition.

Sound design and music further enhance the atmosphere. Energetic tracks accompany each round, maintaining a sense of excitement and momentum, while sound effects emphasize the physical interactions between players and the environment. The combination of audio and visuals ensures that even moments of failure feel entertaining rather than frustrating. Losing a round often comes with a sense of humor, as the game encourages players to laugh at the chaos rather than dwell on defeat.

The multiplayer aspect is where Fall Guys truly thrives. Whether playing alone or with friends, the game creates memorable moments through shared unpredictability. Large matches amplify the sense of chaos, with players constantly interacting, intentionally or otherwise, as they navigate each challenge. The addition of cross-platform play expands the player base and makes it easier to connect with others, reinforcing the game’s identity as a social experience. It is a game that benefits from being played in bursts, where each session offers a mix of competition and lighthearted fun.

Despite its strengths, the game does face some limitations. Over time, repetition can become noticeable, particularly if players encounter the same stages frequently. While updates and new content help address this, maintaining variety remains an ongoing challenge. The transition to a free-to-play model also introduced monetization elements such as cosmetic purchases and seasonal progression systems, which may not appeal to everyone. Although these features do not impact gameplay directly, they can affect the overall perception of progression and reward.

Another point of contention is the role of randomness. While unpredictability is a core part of the experience, it can sometimes lead to situations where outcomes feel outside the player’s control. Being eliminated due to crowd dynamics or unexpected interactions can be frustrating, especially in later rounds where stakes are higher. For some players, this adds to the excitement and keeps the game from becoming overly competitive; for others, it may diminish the sense of fairness.

Ultimately, Fall Guys succeeds because it understands exactly what it wants to be. It does not aim to deliver deep systems or intense competition, but rather a joyful, chaotic experience that is easy to pick up and enjoy. Its combination of accessibility, humor, and unpredictability makes it a standout in the multiplayer space, offering something that feels both familiar and refreshingly different. While it may not hold every player’s attention in the long term, it excels as a game that can be returned to again and again for moments of lighthearted entertainment.

Rating: 8/10

review:
[b]It’s a shame that Fall Guys released as it did, as good as it was, because now it’s an even better game than it ever has been, save for a few, non-core gameplay, aspects, yet it’s completely gone from the cultural conscience. In fact, it might as well have never been the biggest game on the planet! But here’s to you Fall Guys, still my favorite Battle Royale all these years on.[/b]

Fall Guys is probably still the best Battle Royale game/mode ever made. It absolutely mogs [url=https://store.steampowered.com/app/578080/PUBG_BATTLEGROUNDS/]PUBG[/url], it makes you forget [url=https://store.steampowered.com/app/939510/Pandemic_Express__Zombie_Escape/#app_reviews_hash]Pandemic Express[/url] ever came out, it survived an Epic Games review bomb unlike [url=https://store.steampowered.com/app/1399780/Spellbreak/]Spellbreak[/url], never got into legal issues like [url=https://store.steampowered.com/app/2016590/Dark_and_Darker/]Dark and Darker[/url], and reminds you of the death of your favorite MOBA, [url=https://store.steampowered.com/app/504370/Battlerite/]Battlerite[/url].

Jokes aside, Fall Guys was and still is a wildly fun game that makes high stakes gameplay continually feel simple, yet challenging, and silly environments like they’re full of adrenaline boosting peril. There’s not really any other game which does it like Fall Guys does, and makes losing so much fun, as well as getting into another round of gameplay so addicting and so easy!

In my mind, Fall Guys’ greatest shortcoming is that the game is segmented between all of these different queue options now: Ranked knockout, Knockout, Roll Call Ranked, 3,2,1 Space, Creator Spotlight, etc. besides the last one, do you have any idea what the ♥♥♥♥ any of these actually are? It’s a complete mess, in my opinion. And as Fall Guys ages, this will present a problem for it, as I have discussed continually in my reviews for games such as [url=https://steamcommunity.com/id/ShynyMagikarp/recommended/1301210/]Knockout City[/url].

But I’ve yet to touch on Fall Guys’ absolute greatest strength bar none… the course creator. You’d think from looking at Steam News Posts that Fall Guys has been untouched for years now. But that is far from the truth. Over the last two years, the course creator has gone from barebones into a more complete and satisfying experience allowing you to make all kinds of rounds you dreamed about when the game first came out, and with sufficient tool and part capacities to let you make things for mass lobbies, as well as fun things to play with the boys at your [i]in-houses[/i].

Now, with the course creator, Fall Guys is not only a game of silly fun, it’s also a pretty decent creative outlet that not only gives you the dreamlike experience of being a contestant on Wipeout or Takeshi’s Caste, but also letting you go behind the scenes to manufacture the chaos yourself.

With a strong creator tool, lots of developer made levels, cosmetics galore, emotes, and tons of replayability, I still recommend Fall Guys above pretty much any other Battle Royale, and I will occasionally be playing this one still until someday I can hopefully finally get this last pesky achievement.

[b]9/10[/b]

Fall Guys is an absolute blast. The game is colorful, chaotic, and genuinely fun in a way that is hard not to enjoy. The variety of rounds keeps things fresh, the competition is entertaining, and there is something deeply satisfying about being the last bean standing after surviving pure mayhem. Mediatonic built a party game that is easy to pick up and hard to stop playing.

Only one complaint:
1- I got banned. Apparently being too good at the game is a problem.
8/10 one of the most fun and chaotic party games out there. Just do not be too good or they will come for you.

review:
There is something almost perversely elegant about Fall Guys: Ultimate Knockout—a game that, on its surface, masquerades as chaotic slapstick, yet reveals itself, upon closer inspection, to be a remarkably distilled study in modern multiplayer design.

At first glance, one might dismiss Fall Guys as little more than a candy-colored collision of jellybean avatars and kinetic absurdity. But to do so would be to overlook its quietly sophisticated understanding of human behavior. Each round—whether a race, survival challenge, or team-based ordeal—functions as a microcosm of competition itself, compressing tension, cooperation, and betrayal into brisk, digestible bursts. The result is a gameplay loop that feels almost architectural in its precision: simple inputs, complex outcomes.

What is particularly striking is the game’s rejection of the hyper-optimized, reflex-driven ethos that dominates much of the battle royale genre. Instead, Fall Guys embraces friction—literal and metaphorical. Movement is deliberately imprecise, physics are gleefully unreliable, and victory often hinges less on mechanical mastery than on adaptability within disorder. In this way, the game achieves something rare: it democratizes success. Skill matters, certainly, but so does timing, positioning, and, occasionally, sheer luck. It is competition reimagined as spectacle rather than hierarchy.

Aesthetically, the game operates in a space that feels both nostalgic and subversive. Its visual language—bright palettes, rounded forms, exaggerated animations—evokes children’s television, yet it is deployed in the service of a system that is quietly ruthless. Elimination is constant, progress is fleeting, and triumph is singular. This juxtaposition creates a tonal dissonance that is, paradoxically, the game’s greatest strength. It invites players to invest emotionally while simultaneously encouraging them not to take failure too seriously.

Moreover, the social dimension of Fall Guys cannot be overstated. Whether played solo or in groups, it generates moments of shared absurdity that feel almost performative. One does not merely play Fall Guys; one participates in it, contributing to a collective narrative of near-misses, improbable recoveries, and spectacular blunders. In an era where many multiplayer experiences feel isolating despite their scale, this sense of communal presence is quietly profound.

Ultimately, Fall Guys: Ultimate Knockout succeeds not because it is complex, but because it understands exactly how much complexity to conceal beneath its surface. It is a game that appears frivolous yet is meticulously constructed, that feels chaotic yet is carefully balanced, and that invites laughter while sustaining genuine competitive engagement. In short, it is not merely a party game—it is a remarkably astute commentary on the nature of play itself.

A esto se le suman los nuevos modos que van añadiendo por temporadas, lo que mantiene el juego vivo y hace que siempre haya algo nuevo por probar. Sin duda, es una experiencia bastante adictiva.

review:
Купил Fall Guys с мыслью: “это же просто весёлые колобки, сейчас расслаблюсь”.

Прошло 5 минут — я уже эмоционально разрушен персонажем в костюме хот-дога, который знает про паркур больше, чем я про свою жизнь.

Эта игра выглядит мило.
Это её главная ложь.

Первые раунды:
— “ха-ха, я упал, смешно”

Позже:
— я падаю
— я злюсь
— я падаю с осознанием смысла жизни

Игра очень быстро показывает, что дружба — это временное явление.

Сегодня вы вместе прыгаете через вращающиеся молоты…

завтра ты лично наблюдаешь, как твой “друг” в скине динозавра толкает тебя в пропасть с абсолютно спокойной душой.

Физика тут не физика.

Это отдельная религия хаоса, где ты либо летишь идеально…
либо улетаешь так, будто тебя выгнали из реальности за плохое поведение.

Финальные раунды — это уже не игра.

Это психологический экзамен:
— выдержишь ли ты, что тебя почти дошли до победы
— и всё равно кто-то в последний момент случайно превращает тебя в зрителя

Итог:
10/10

Игра научила меня важному:
если ты видишь перед собой прыжок “совсем лёгкий” — значит там тебя уже ждут 40 человек с одинаковой идеей испортить тебе день.

Сегодня вы вместе прыгаете через вращающиеся молоты завтра ты лично наблюдаешь, как твой друг в скине динозавра толкает тебя в пропасть с абсолютно спокойной душой. Физика тут не физика. Это отдельная религия хаоса, где ты либо летишь идеально либо улетаешь так, будто тебя выгнали из реальности за плохое поведение. Финальные раунды это уже не игра. review_english: I bought Fall Guys with the thought that these are just funny koloboks, now I’ll relax. 5 minutes have passed and I am already emotionally destroyed by a character in a hot dog costume who knows more about parkour than I do about my life. This game looks cute. This is her main lie. The first rounds haha, I fell, funny. Later I fall, I get angry, I fall with the realization of the meaning of life. The game very quickly shows that friendship is a temporary phenomenon. Today you jump over rotating hammers together, tomorrow you personally watch as your friend in a dinosaur skin pushes you into the abyss with an absolutely calm soul. Physics is not physics here. This is a separate religion of chaos, where you either fly perfectly or fly away as if you were kicked out of reality for bad behavior. The final rounds are no longer a game. This is a psychological test, can you stand it, that you almost reached victory and still someone at the last moment accidentally turns you into a spectator Result 10 10 The game taught me something important: if you see a very easy jump in front of you, it means there are already 40 people waiting for you with the same idea of ​​ruining your day.
